children.heading: .—General
children.label: a .—General
children.text: The board of directors is the governing body of the corporation.

The Librarian of Congress (hereafter in this chapter referred to as the “Librarian”) is an ex officio nonvoting member of the board. Not later than 90 days after the date of the enactment of this chapter, the Librarian shall appoint the directors to the board in accordance with paragraph (2).

children.label: B
children.text: Each director shall be a United States citizen.

children.label: C
children.text: At least 8 directors shall be knowledgeable or experienced in sound recording production, distribution, preservation, or restoration, including two who are sitting members of the National Recording Preservation Board. These 8 directors shall, to the extent practicable, represent diverse points of view from the sound recording community.

A director is not an employee of the Library of Congress and appointment to the board does not constitute appointment as an officer or employee of the United States Government for the purpose of any law of the United States.
The terms of office of the directors are 4 years.
A vacancy on the board shall be filled in the manner in which the original appointment was made.

children.heading: .—Chair
children.label: c .—Chair
children.text: The Librarian shall appoint one of the directors as the initial chair of the board for a 2-year term. Thereafter, the chair shall be appointed and removed in accordance with the bylaws of the corporation.

children.heading: .—Quorum
children.label: d .—Quorum
children.text: The number of directors constituting a quorum of the board shall be established under the bylaws of the corporation.

children.heading: .—Meetings
children.label: e .—Meetings
children.text: The board shall meet at the call of the Librarian for regularly scheduled meetings.

children.heading: .—Reimbursement of Expenses
children.label: f .—Reimbursement of Expenses
children.text: Directors shall serve without compensation but may receive travel expenses, including per diem in lieu of subsistence, in accordance with sections 5702 and 5703 of title 5.

children.heading: .—Liability of Directors
children.label: g .—Liability of Directors
children.text: Directors are not personally liable, except for gross negligence.
